title: hieracium subramosum subsp. incurrens
intro: Hieracium subramosum subsp. incurrens, a lesser-known species of the Hieracium genus, is a type of flowering plant commonly referred to as Hawkweed. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
